title: DeepSeek's Rise: The Illusion of Excluding China from AI Governance
content:
At the start of last year, the inadequacy of global AI governance was exposed. A Chinese lab called DeepSeek unveiled a powerful AI system, shattering the delusion that China could be left out of safety regulations. Despite an American-led effort to deprive China of the most powerful computer chips, the DeepSeek model was highly advanced. It was almost as close to the Promethean goal of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) as its western rivals. Other Chinese tech firms soon rolled out similar models.

Experts quickly spelled out the consequences of this setback. An international safety collaboration that excluded China would be pointless. If even only one or two of these projects design harmful AGIs, it could pose a serious existential threat to humanity. A lasting safety regime cannot be spearheaded by companies alone. Furthermore, it absolutely cannot ignore China.

This brings us back to the broader challenges of handling cyber threats. Proposed solutions, such as restricted-release policies or government-dictated access, fail to respect the lessons taught by the brief history of artificial intelligence. A lasting, legitimate framework must involve comprehensive international cooperation. Only through inclusive dialogue and shared responsibility can the world safely navigate the future of AGI.
